subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 25
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is a reptile that has a hard shell on its back?
questions.explanation: Turtles are reptiles covered by a bony or cartilaginous shell that acts as a shield against predators.
questions.hint: It moves slowly and has a hard back.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital of Japan and one of the most populous cities in the world.

questions.q: Which part of the plant helps anchor it in the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots grow deep into the ground to keep the plant stable and to absorb water and minerals.
questions.hint: They are the 'feet' of the plant.

questions.q: What do we call a person who flies an airplane?
questions.explanation: A pilot is a person trained to operate the flight controls of an aircraft.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your teeth?
questions.explanation: Regular brushing is essential to prevent cavities and keep your gums healthy.
questions.hint: Do this in the morning and before bed.

questions.q: Which community helper helps to keep our city clean?
questions.explanation: Sanitation workers collect garbage and recycling to keep our communities clean and healthy.

questions.q: What is the name of the planet known for its beautiful rings?
questions.explanation: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun and is famous for its massive, visible ring system.

questions.q: Which of these is a source of light and heat for Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star at the center of our solar system that provides light and heat to support life on Earth.
questions.hint: It is the center of our solar system.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water turns to steam at 100 degrees Celsius.

questions.q: Which season is known for falling leaves and cooler weather?
questions.explanation: Autumn (or fall) is the season when trees shed their leaves and temperatures begin to drop.
